executive_summary: Validated market with 12+ competitors (BetterHelp, Talkspace, Brightline, Kooth globally; Terapify, Sanarai, Mindy in LATAM) but dominated by well-funded US incumbents (Teladoc/BetterHelp $950M rev, Talkspace $800M+ acquisition); major gaps in Spanish-language teen-specific AI triage and LATAM-affordable pricing ($20-30/session vs $260-436/mo US) create a defensible niche — score 62/100.
score_reasoning:
Score: 62/100
- Market Validation Signal: 13/20 (12 competitors found: 7 direct [BetterHelp/TeenCounseling, Talkspace, Brightline, Kooth, Terapify, Sanarai, Mindy], 2 indirect [Wysa, Woebot-deceased], 3 adjacent [Doctoralia, Go Doctor, CUX]; solidly in 6-15 tier with 7 direct + 2 indirect)
- Incumbent Weakness: 3/20 (strongest competitor: BetterHelp/Teladoc — public company NYSE:TDOC, $2.5B parent revenue, BetterHelp segment $950M/yr, 7800+ Trustpilot reviews; runner-up: Talkspace — public company NASDAQ:TALK, being acquired for $800M+) [INVERTED: low points = dominant incumbent]
  INVERSION CHECK: 3 points means dominant incumbent exists, hard to compete. Consistent with evidence: YES — BetterHelp is a subsidiary of a public company with >$50M funding and >1000 reviews. Flag 'dominant-incumbent-found' set.
- Market Gap Evidence: 17/20 (7 gaps identified from reviews/research: (1) no Spanish/culturally-adapted teen content [academic papers + focus groups + user reviews], (2) US pricing prohibitive for LATAM [$260-436/mo vs $20-30/session avg], (3) generic non-personalized content for teens [focus group + app reviews], (4) no AI triage/preliminary diagnostic [gap across all platforms], (5) privacy/data concerns [FTC settlement + Trustpilot], (6) therapist matching/wait times [BBB + Trustpilot + Reddit], (7) no LATAM-specific teen platform [market gap]; 3+ thematically related to LATAM teen niche)
- Pricing Intelligence: 16/20 (pricing found for 6+ competitors: BetterHelp/TeenCounseling $280-400/mo, Talkspace $276-436/mo, Calmerry $228-360/mo, Terapify ~$30/session, Hola Therapy $115/session, Doctoralia $25-50/session; tier detail for BetterHelp, Talkspace, Calmerry)
- Failure Intelligence: 13/20 (3 dead competitors: Woebot [$123M raised, shut down June 2025, FDA regulatory barriers], Mindstrong [$160M raised, shut down March 2024, funding/unit economics], Yara AI [shut down Nov 2025, safety concerns]; 2 readable post-mortems [Woebot STAT article, Mindstrong press coverage]; 5+ churn signals from BetterHelp [TeenCounseling 2.4/5 Trustpilot, BBB complaints, FTC $7.8M settlement])
Total: 13 + 3 + 17 + 16 + 13 = 62

pricing.range: Covered by employer/insurance plans; no public cash-pay pricing
Free to end users; government contracts ($680M California deal)

pricing_benchmark.model: mixed (subscription in US, per-session in LATAM)
pricing_benchmark.notes: Massive pricing gap between US platforms ($65-109/week) and LATAM platforms ($15-30/session). LATAM mid-point ~$25-30/session is the target benchmark. Kooth and some NGO-backed services are free (B2G model).

evidence.quote: Teladoc projects $2.47B-$2.59B revenue in 2026; BetterHelp segment revenue $950M in 2025, guided down 7% to 0.5% for 2026. Market cap $829M as of Feb 2026.

Woebot Health shut down its CBT-based therapy chatbot on June 30, 2025 after raising $123M. Founder cited FDA regulatory requirements as primary reason — cost and challenge of marketing authorization made it unviable.
California inks $680 million deal with Brightline and Kooth for virtual pediatric mental health services for all residents aged 25 and younger, available in English and Spanish.
